# @zakkster/lite-headless / meter

ARIA `role="meter"`. A scalar measurement within a known range.

## When to use meter vs progress

| | meter | progress |
|---|---|---|
| ARIA role | `meter` | `progressbar` |
| Semantic | "this value sits at X" | "this task is X% done" |
| Indeterminate | no | yes |
| Examples | vote share, fuel level, gauge | uploads, multi-step wizards |

For **Twitch Prediction / Poll vote shares**: meter is the right
semantic. The value isn't a task in progress; it's a measured share
of total votes.

## Quick example

```js
import { createMeter } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/meter";

const meter = createMeter({
    value: 0.42,
    min: 0,
    max: 1,
    label: "Vote share ‚Äî Yes",
});
meter.attachRoot(rootEl);
meter.attachFill(fillEl);

// Live updates:
meter.setValue(0.51);
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `value` | `0` | Current value (clamped to `[min..max]`) |
| `min` | `0` | Range minimum |
| `max` | `1` | Range maximum |
| `low` | `null` | If value < low, region is "low" |
| `high` | `null` | If value > high, region is "high" |
| `optimum` | `null` | Defines which region is the "good" one |
| `label` | `null` | aria-label (only set if root doesn't already have one) |
| `valueText` | `null` | aria-valuetext (human-readable override) |

`max` must be strictly greater than `min`; the constructor throws otherwise.

## State (computed per HTML meter spec)

When `low` / `high` / `optimum` are configured, `meter.state()` returns
one of:

- `"optimum"` ‚Äî value is in the optimum region
- `"sub-optimum"` ‚Äî value is in a neighbor region
- `"low"` ‚Äî value is on the opposite side from optimum

When no thresholds are set, state is always `"optimum"`.

The state mirrors browser behavior for native `<meter low high optimum>`:

- If `optimum` is in the mid band: both lo and hi extremes are "sub-optimum"
- If `optimum` is below `low`: the mid band is "sub-optimum"; the hi extreme is "low"
- If `optimum` is above `high`: the mid band is "sub-optimum"; the lo extreme is "low"

## API

```ts
{
    value(): number
    min(): number
    max(): number
    fraction(): number     // (value - min) / (max - min), clamped [0..1]
    state(): "optimum" | "sub-optimum" | "low"

    setValue(v: number): void
    setValueText(t: string | null): void

    attachRoot(el): off
    attachFill(el): off    // optional ‚Äî paints --meter + data-zone on a fill child

    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

### Root (`[data-meter-root]`)

- `role="meter"` (only if not pre-set)
- `aria-valuenow="<value>"`
- `aria-valuemin="<min>"`
- `aria-valuemax="<max>"`
- `aria-valuetext="<text>"` (when `valueText` provided)
- `aria-label="<label>"` (only if root doesn't already have `aria-label`/`aria-labelledby`)
- `data-zone="optimum|sub-optimum|low"`
- CSS variable `--meter: <0..1 fraction>`

### Fill (`[data-meter-fill]`)

- `data-zone="<same as root>"`
- CSS variable `--meter: <0..1 fraction>`

The fill is purely a CSS hook. Consumer renders it however they want:

```css
/* Horizontal fill bar */
[data-meter-fill] {
    transform-origin: left center;
    transform: scaleX(var(--meter, 0));
}

/* Or width-based */
[data-meter-fill] { width: calc(var(--meter, 0) * 100%); }

/* Color per state */
[data-meter-fill][data-zone="optimum"]      { background: var(--green); }
[data-meter-fill][data-zone="sub-optimum"]  { background: var(--amber); }
[data-meter-fill][data-zone="low"]          { background: var(--red);   }
```

## Wrapper (`<lite-meter>`)

```html
<lite-meter value="0.42" min="0" max="1" low="0.25" high="0.75"
            label="Yes vote share" value-text="42 percent of votes">
    <div data-meter-fill></div>
</lite-meter>
```

Attributes:
- `value`, `min`, `max` ‚Äî numeric (default 0, 0, 1)
- `low`, `high`, `optimum` ‚Äî numeric (optional)
- `label` ‚Äî `aria-label` text
- `value-text` ‚Äî `aria-valuetext` override

Reactive: `value` and `value-text` attributes flow through bidirectionally.

Host accessors:
- `host.value`, `host.fraction`, `host.state`
- `host.setValue(v)`, `host.setValueText(t)`
- `host._meterInstance` (advanced)

## CSS contract

Follows `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. `data-zone` is a Class-3 enum.
`data-meter-root`/`data-meter-fill` are Class-4 slot markers.
